ActionItems: Negotiate rent renewal to stay at or below 750 to protect monthly net income.
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Break-even analysis for the shared ceramics studio space at the Higashiyama Craft Collective, modeling membership dues against fixed and variable operating costs ahead of the rent renewal.

CurrentState.Note: Membership dues are the primary revenue driver; drop-ins and equipment sales cover variable supply costs.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: The studio breaks even at 10 members under the current rent; each rent increase of 100 raises the break-even point by roughly one member.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: (NewTotalExpenses 2260 - non-membership revenue 830) / Rate 145 = 9.86, rounded up to 10 members
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Net income falls from 1280 to 1180; still sustainable with current membership.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to 750

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Net income falls from 1280 to 1080; requires retaining at least 11 members to stay profitable.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Rent increases to 850
